title: EOE One More Final Asukas cold look
display_title: EOE One More Final Asukas cold look
wikitext:
[]: EOE: One More Final - Asuka's "cold" look.             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Jan 05, 2011 6:50 pm Thread in itself is absurd, because my initial line of thought was proven wrong. I propably was prejudiced because in the German sub (and so far this is the only one I've seen) Asuka's voice for the last line is not cold or monotonous, but simply sounds as if she ois indeed feeling sick. I would even say ot sounds a little whiny. I guess that led me to see the whole scene in a wrong light, but I've been proven wrong.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Wed Jan 05, 2011 6:57 pm I have no idea how one could not describe the gaze as cold. Even the colours chosen reflect this

[]: Re: EOE: One More Final - Asuka's "cold" look.             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jan 05, 2011 7:02 pm die-yng wrote: I know we already have a thread concerning the "strangling scene", but I felt it was neccessary to start a new thread, because I really only want to talk about one part in the let's say "more negative" (totally wrong) interpretation of said scene.

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Jan 05, 2011 7:36 pm Of course this would make sense. But your interpretation is only partly true. If she says this about Shinji, then her tender gesture a minute earlier would make no sense. "Oh, he's trying to strangle me, to make him stop I'll show him I care about him." (Again, he's just trying to strangle her, still she reacts this way) And then the fact that he weeps after all that's happened makes her despise him? Yeah that makes much more sense. I have no idea how one could not describe the gaze as cold. Even the colours chosen reflect this

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jan 05, 2011 8:00 pm die-yng wrote: And then the fact that he weeps after all that's happened makes her despise him?

[]:             Quote Post by die-yng » Wed Jan 05, 2011 8:37 pm Azathoth wrote: Why not? Asuka always wanted Shinji to "man up" act the way she would - or at least act, instead of just sitting around and letting others act on him. I would think it's the revelation that he's still a pitiful wreck that weighs most strongly into her comment.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Wed Jan 05, 2011 9:17 pm die-yng wrote: Now if she lived through similar visions and decided to become corporeal, than isn't it likely that she came to similar "enlightement" about life as well?

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Jan 06, 2011 6:49 am My impression is that disgust is foremost on Asuka's mind throughout Human Instrumentality and beyond. Her defining traits are her individualism, her strong personality and her forceful detachment from others (even though this hurts her as well). As such, she is the exact opposite of Instrumentality, and it is no surprise that she is the first to reattain her individuality by her own strength of will. With Asuka established as the personification of the "other", who is undone by Instrumentality, it is of course cruel irony that Arael probes her innermost personality during its attack, which renders her useless afterwards. Additionally, her melding with her mother during the first half of EoE may appear to be a victory for her, but ultimately it was her undoing. By becoming one with her mother inside Unit-02, Asuka (literally) destroyed herself. Asuka's disgust stems not only from this, but also from having experienced Instrumentality, that which is the total opposite of who she is. I believe, therefore, that the Asuka we see in the final scene is numb with disgust. When Shinji starts strangling her, this elicits her to acknowledge his presence by caressing his face. As her eye turns towards him, she realizes that she is separate from him (rather than that they are part of each other, like in Instrumentality), and recognizes him. And while he breaks down in tears, she remembers his thoughts about her during Instrumentality, including the masturbation, the earlier strangling scene, and loads and loads of Shinji's self-loathing. They had been one for a short time, and she did not like being privy to his inner thoughts, emotions and self-disgust. In fact, she did not like being one with anyone. It disgusts her. "Kimochi warui", as a general term of disgust, thus reflects this numb disgust at being one with others, at what it has done to her, at others in general (and at Shinji specifically, because he is the only other), and finally at herself. Shinji breaks down in self-loathing, and Asuka becomes aware that she feels the same way about herself, too. Asuka's disgust ("Kimochi warui") is therefore numbly directed at literally everything . This is supported by the previous uses of the term "kimochi warui" in the NGE series. (This is not my own observation, but I unfortunately cannot remember where I found it, and who I should credit.) The first is the very first time either Asuka enters the entry plug and is submerged in LCL. (I believe it wasn't the Gaghiel battle, because they didn't have access to LCL at sea.) Because LCL represents being one with another (the soul inside the EVA in this case), Asuka's disgust illustrates her opposition to Instrumentality. The second instance of "kimochi warui" is in one of the later episodes, shortly before the Arael mindrape, in a scene added in the extended version of the episode. Asuka is seen complaining to herself about using the same bath, food and home as "Misato and Baka Shinji". Again, this shows her strong repulsion at being intimate to others. (I apologize for being unable to refer to specific scenes and episodes for these two examples.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 06, 2011 2:12 pm Jornophelanthas wrote: The first is the very first time either Asuka enters the entry plug and is submerged in LCL.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Thu Jan 06, 2011 2:52 pm Mr. Tines wrote: The first is Shinji reacting to being submerged in LCL in episode 1.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Thu Jan 06, 2011 3:03 pm This thread is unbearably stupid.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Jan 06, 2011 3:05 pm ^And did that work to improve matters? I think not.

[]:             Quote Post by supershinjiasukashipper » Thu Jan 06, 2011 9:34 pm A brilliant incite Jornophelanthas. We need more of your face around here.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 07, 2011 7:36 am die-yng wrote: Because her expression is exactly the same as before! D'uh!

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Fri Jan 07, 2011 9:12 am Xard wrote: When she looks up to sky Asuka looks traumatized, shell shocked and catatonic and whatnot. When she rolls her eye down to gaze at Shinji she is personification of contempt.

[]:             Quote Post by Baz » Fri Jan 07, 2011 9:33 am Those tears falling on Asuka's face always pull me right out of the movie. It is as if her face is flat, or even worse, that the tears are falling on the cel itself. Would it really have cost that much to animate the tears rolling off her face, to give the illusion that her face is more than just paint on plastic?

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Fri Jan 07, 2011 10:06 am Baz wrote: Those tears falling on Asuka's face always pull me right out of the movie. It is as if her face is flat, or even worse, that the tears are falling on the cel itself. Would it really have cost that much to animate the tears rolling off her face, to give the illusion that her face is more than just paint on plastic?

[]:             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Jan 07, 2011 10:54 am Baz wrote: Would it really have cost that much to animate the tears rolling off her face, to give the illusion that her face is more than just paint on plastic?

[]:             Quote Post by The Forgotten Knight » Fri Jan 07, 2011 9:33 pm She looks pretty shell shocked traumatized from the whole Instrumentality Project to me. After becoming one with everyone in existence I can't say that I blame her. As for the tears not rolling down the side of the face I'm pretty sure thats just a production oversight versus intentional symbolism. The reason it stands out is because tears don't behave that way naturally.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Jan 08, 2011 3:39 am The Forgotten Knight wrote: She looks pretty shell shocked traumatized from the whole Instrumentality Project to me.
